perturbation.original_text: "Global Contract Number" means the reference number shown on the first page of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "Global Contract Number" means a number assigned to each Purchase Order by Bellicum and unrelated to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the Global Contract Number as being shown on the first page of the agreement. The modified text redefines it as a number assigned to each Purchase Order, creating a contradiction as to what this number refers to. This is relevant when attempting to place a purchase order under the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Each Purchase Order will specify the MB Global Contract Number assigned to this Agreement, the volumes of Miltenyi Product(s) ordered, the desired Delivery date(s) the Miltenyi Products are to be made available to Bellicum for pick-up by Bellicum's designated carrier or freight forwarder, the relevant ship-to address, and any special shipping instructions. Bellicum will order Miltenyi Product in a defined number of units, subject to reasonable minimum order size requirements that may vary according to product type.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined "Global Contract Number" as being located on the first page of the agreement. The changed text redefines it as a number assigned to each Purchase Order by Bellicum and unrelated to the agreement. The contradicted text states that each Purchase Order will specify the "MB Global Contract Number assigned to this Agreement". The change causes a direct conflict because the number used on the purchase order is no longer connected to the agreement itself, leading to confusion about which Global Contract Number should be used, and whether there even exists a number assigned to the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: There is no binding forecasting obligation for Research Grade Products, except (if applicable) as otherwise explicitly agreed in a Module.
perturbation.changed_text: There is a binding forecasting obligation for Research Grade Products, regardless of what is mentioned in the module.
perturbation.explanation: The original text exempts Research Grade Products from binding forecasting obligations unless otherwise agreed upon in a Module. The modified text states that there is a binding forecasting obligation for Research Grade Products, directly contradicting the original statement. This creates uncertainty about whether Bellicum is obligated to provide binding forecasts for Research Grade Products, which impacts inventory planning and procurement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In order to assist Miltenyi with its capacity, procurement and production planning, and as a general framework for forecasting Bellicum's orders of Miltenyi Products (where more specific parameters may be set forth in a given Module), Bellicum agrees to provide Miltenyi with rolling forecasts of Bellicum's (and its Subcontractors' and Licensees') anticipated quantity requirements for Miltenyi Products in the Forecast Territory during the Term of this Agreement, in accordance with the provisions of this Section 5.1 (each, a "Forecast"). There is a binding forecasting obligation for Research Grade Products, regardless of what is mentioned in the module. Any modified forecasting terms and conditions for a particular Bellicum Product or Bellicum Program that supplement this Article 5 will be set forth in the Module applicable to that Bellicum Product or Bellicum Program. All of the Forecasts provided under this Agreement will break down the demand of Miltenyi Products on a product-by-product (expressed in number of units) and manufacturing country-by-manufacturing country basis (i.e., Forecast Territory only) and substantially follow the mutually agreed Miltenyi forecast sheet, as attached hereto in Exhibit C 1-3. All Forecasts provided by Bellicum will be good faith estimates of Bellicum's anticipated quantity requirements for Miltenyi Products during the relevant period. Bellicum agrees to use [...***...] in preparing all Forecasts provided hereunder to minimize variances between Forecasts. Each Forecast shall be duly signed by an authorized representative of Bellicum (or Bellicum's designee on behalf of Bellicum) and submitted in writing to Miltenyi, by mail, email or facsimile, and shall supersede prior Forecasts to the extent the Forecast overlaps with prior Forecasts.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'There is no binding forecasting obligation for Research Grade Products, except (if applicable) as otherwise explicitly agreed in a Module.' The changed text states 'There is a binding forecasting obligation for Research Grade Products, regardless of what is mentioned in the module.' This is a direct contradiction. The contradicted text discusses forecasting obligations, including those for Research Grade Products. The changed text removes the exception for Research Grade Products, creating a clear and direct conflict, therefore creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party will execute, acknowledge and deliver such further instruments, and do all such other acts, as may be necessary or appropriate in order to carry out the purposes and intent of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Party will execute, acknowledge and deliver such further instruments, and do all such other acts, as may be necessary or appropriate in order to carry out the purposes and intent of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires each party to execute the further instruments. The modified text forbids each party from executing the instruments, directly contradicting the orginal meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Party will execute, acknowledge and deliver such further instruments, and do all such other acts, as may be necessary or appropriate in order to carry out the purposes and intent of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'Neither Party will execute...instruments', directly contradicting the original text which stated 'Each Party will execute...instruments'. Furthermore, the changed text is identical to the contradicted text, demonstrating a structural flaw where the same clause exists in multiple locations with opposing implications, creating a direct contradiction in the obligations of the parties, thus fitting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
